articles.title: Record gas price surge fuels sharp rise in U.S. inflation - Honolulu Star-Advertiser
articles.description: WASHINGTON >> U.S. consumer prices increased by the most in nearly four years in March as the war with Iran led to a record surge in the cost of gasoline and diesel, dealing a blow to President Donald Trump, whose approval ratings have been falling because of…

articles.title: Something just changed with Palantir stock, and investors noticed - thestreet.com
articles.description: A shift in Palantir's story has investors on both sides wondering whether it's time to buy.

articles.title: Sam Altman’s Home Attacked With Molotov Cocktail, Suspect Taken Into Custody - WSJ
articles.description: The individual allegedly made threats at OpenAI’s San Francisco headquarters, company says

articles.title: Developer of Massachusetts offshore wind farm sues to stop turbine manufacturer from walking away - AP News
articles.description: The developer of a major Massachusetts offshore wind farm is suing its turbine manufacturer seeking to force it to stay with the project. Vineyard Wind filed a lawsuit Wednesday in Massachusetts against GE Renewables. That comes after the parent company for G…

articles.title: Nike names 20-year veteran new chief innovation officer - OregonLive.com
articles.description: Andy Caine succeeds Tony Bignell as the company preps for a pivotal product showcase this fall.

articles.title: Fed Seeks Details on US Banks’ Exposure to Private Credit Firms - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: The Federal Reserve is asking major US banks for details about their exposure to private credit following a surge in redemptions from the funds and a rise in troubled loans in the industry, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

articles.title: Smucker’s offers Artemis II crewmembers a lifetime supply of Uncrustables upon splashdown - Fortune
articles.description: The crew aboard the Artemis II requested Uncrustables upon their return to Earth. The Smucker’s brand just offered them a lifetime supply.

articles.title: Judge blocks new state rules that ban sale of smokable hemp - KUT
articles.description: A Travis County judge agreed with plaintiffs that some of the rules that took effect March 31 were causing irreparable harm.

articles.title: IMF chief concerned about cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's AI model Mythos: "Time is not our friend" - CBS News
articles.description: Kristalina Georgieva, the head of the International Monetary Fund, called on key institutions to work together to manage cybersecurity risks.

articles.title: FCC Router Ban Is Bad News for These Brands. Is Yours on the List? - PCMag
articles.description: Speedtest.net cataloged the most popular router brands in the US. If they fail to shift production to the US or secure an exemption, they could face trouble licensing new models with the FCC.

articles.title: Wall Street ignores rising inflation, falling consumer confidence - thestreet.com
articles.description: Wall Street has unequivocally lost its mind (not that it hasn't before).

articles.title: Iran War Hits US Economy With More Inflation, Record-Low Sentiment - Bloomberg.com
articles.description: The war in Iran has fueled the biggest jump in US inflation in nearly four years and sent consumer sentiment tumbling to a record low. Economists say that’s just the beginning.

articles.title: Kentucky bourbon giants may merge - Axios
articles.description: Booze brands are in decline, thanks to a sober-minded Gen Z.

articles.description: CoreWeave announces a multi-year deal with Anthropic to power Claude AI models, expanding scalable, high-performance infrastructure for enterprise AI deployment

articles.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue - NPR
articles.description: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.

articles.title: Gallup: Gen Z growing more negative toward AI - Higher Ed Dive
articles.description: Most respondents expressed concern that AI designed to speed up tasks would "make learning more difficult.”
